phenotypes.label: Pituitary neoplasm with hypopituitarism
phenotypes.description: Patients with a pituitary neoplasm (pituitary mass) and consequent pituitary hormone deficiencies. Co-coded features include panhypopituitarism, diabetes insipidus, and reproductive endocrine symptoms, with nonspecific complaints such as fatigue and incoordination. Metabolic syndrome and common cardiometabolic comorbidities appear in the background.

phenotypes.label: Aortic and intracranial aneurysms
phenotypes.description: Aneurysmal disease across vascular beds, notably thoracic and abdominal aortic aneurysms as well as intracranial/cerebral aneurysms, with related intracerebral vascular findings and subarachnoid hemorrhage. Associated dysphagia and esophageal stricture may reflect mass effect from thoracic aortic dilation, while tracheostomy status and transient liver enzyme elevations likely reflect comorbid critical illness rather than a primary diagnosis.

phenotypes.label: Benign prostatic hyperplasia and LUTS
phenotypes.description: Men with benign prostatic enlargement and bladder outlet obstruction, presenting with nocturia, urinary frequency, incomplete emptying, and urinary retention. Elevated PSA and bladder neck obstruction are frequent; erectile dysfunction, hypogonadism, and occasional chronic prostatitis appear as associated male urologic comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Solid malignancies and mass lesions
phenotypes.description: Solid malignancies with mass lesions and lymphadenopathy dominate, including bone, breast, soft tissue, and pulmonary neoplasms and lung nodules. Associated findings include platelet disorders with petechiae and head/neck pain syndromes, consistent with oncologic involvement or mass effect; reflux esophagitis appears as a frequent comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Melanoma, lung neoplasm, pancreatic cysts
phenotypes.description: Solid tumor pattern centered on cutaneous melanoma, abnormal chest imaging with lung nodules and lower respiratory tract malignancy, and pancreatic cysts/digestive tract neoplasms. Secondary brain metastasis and chronic respiratory failure occur in a subset, with additional solid organ cancers such as renal cell carcinoma and carcinoid tumors represented.

phenotypes.label: Hashimoto hypothyroidism with autoimmune comorbidity
phenotypes.description: Acquired/autoimmune hypothyroidism is central, with Hashimoto thyroiditis and thyroid atrophy prominent. Co-occurring autoimmune and nutritional conditions include celiac disease and pernicious anemia with vitamin B12 deficiency, alongside vitamin D deficiency and osteoporosis; fatigue and malaise are commonly documented.

phenotypes.label: PTSD, depression, and substance use
phenotypes.description: Patients present with PTSD, generalized anxiety, and recurrent major depressive disorder, with some bipolar spectrum diagnoses. Substance use disorders are prominent (alcohol, nicotine, opioids, cocaine/cannabis in remission), and there is frequent coexisting dental disease such as caries, periodontitis, and tooth loss, consistent with psychosocial burden.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not share a clinical theme, mixing common baseline comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity) with scattered, unrelated diagnoses (ocular disorders, neurologic injury, dermatologic and endocrine findings). This lacks a coherent clinical pattern suitable for defining a patient group.

phenotypes.label: Inattentive ADHD with childhood emotional disorders
phenotypes.description: Neurodevelopmental attention disorder dominated by predominantly inattentive ADHD, accompanied by childhood emotional disorders and frequent anxiety/depressive comorbidity. Longitudinal pattern includes adult ADHD and related attention/motor control problems; other findings appear incidental to the core theme.

phenotypes.label: Degenerative spine and chronic pain
phenotypes.description: Broad musculoskeletal pain centered on degenerative spine disease and radiculopathy. Prominent features include chronic low back and neck pain, lumbar/cervical spondylosis, spinal stenosis with neurogenic claudication, sciatica, and disc degeneration, alongside knee/hip osteoarthritis and fibromyalgia. This reflects common back and joint pain presentations across adults.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between gastrointestinal bloating/flatulence with stool abnormalities and a separate cluster of cerebrovascular disease and its sequelae (stroke, cognitive impairment, imbalance). Additional sicca-type symptoms (xerostomia, dry eyes) and stress appear but do not connect these halves into one clinical pathway, so there is no single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Valvular disease and sickle cell
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between non‑rheumatic valvular heart disease (mitral valve prolapse/stenosis/regurgitation, aortic valve disorders) with palpitations and dyspnea, and a separate cluster of sickle cell disease with classic complications (acute chest syndrome, avascular necrosis of the hip, asplenia, iron metabolism disorders) and pulmonary hypertension/cor pulmonale. These represent distinct clinical mechanisms and organ-system drivers, indicating a merged set of unrelated themes rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Retinal disorders and kidney stones
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions divide between retinal disease (age-related macular degeneration, retinal neovascularization, retinal pigment epithelium detachment, central serous chorioretinopathy) and a separate nephrolithiasis/calcium-metabolism cluster (kidney and ureteric stones, hypercalciuria). Additional immune-mediated and immunodeficiency diagnoses (hypogammaglobulinemia, immune thrombocytopenia, lichen planus) further widen the scope, so there is no single coherent clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Cerebral infarction with PFO/ASD
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on acute cerebrovascular disease—ischemic stroke and TIA—with typical post‑stroke sequelae such as aphasia, hemiplegia, facial weakness, and cognitive deficits. Frequent atrial septal defects and patent foramen ovale suggest an embolic mechanism, with occasional gynecologic malignancy hinting at cancer‑associated hypercoagulability. Overall this reflects embolic ischemic stroke with residual deficits.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine acute cerebrovascular disease with hemiplegia, endemic fungal infections (aspergillosis and coccidioidomycosis), and head‑and‑neck malignancies. Additional elements include latent syphilis, high‑risk sexual behavior, and lower‑extremity tendinopathies, without a unifying clinical pathway.

phenotypes.label: Female breast cancer spectrum
phenotypes.description: Centered on female breast malignancy, including quadrant-specific invasive cancers, ductal carcinoma in situ, estrogen receptor–positive/negative tumors, and breast masses with abnormal mammography and calcifications. It also reflects hereditary risk and postsurgical states (acquired absence of breast, absent nipple, reconstruction changes) with sequelae such as lymphedema and bone involvement.

phenotypes.label: Varicose veins with stasis ulcers
phenotypes.description: Peripheral venous insufficiency and varicose veins of the lower extremities with edema, lymphedema, stasis dermatitis, and painful varices are prominent. Complications include chronic leg ulcers, cellulitis, and episodes of superficial or deep venous thrombosis.

phenotypes.label: Glaucoma with cataract and retinal disease
phenotypes.description: Ophthalmic cluster centered on primary open‑angle glaucoma and ocular hypertension with concurrent senile cataracts, tear film insufficiency, and blepharitis. Co‑occurring vitreoretinal pathology is common, including epiretinal membrane, vitreous degeneration, cystoid macular changes, retinal vein occlusion with macular edema, serous detachment, and optic atrophy; some cases involve uveitis‑associated glaucoma. Visual disturbance and vision impairment are frequent, while general cardiometabolic comorbidities are present but not defining.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and obstetric complications
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy across trimesters with both routine and high‑risk obstetric conditions. Findings include hypertensive disorders of pregnancy, RhD negative status, urinary tract infection in pregnancy, threatened preterm labor, placental abnormalities, fetal growth restriction, post‑term or early pregnancy states, and maternal factors such as obesity, anemia, and prior uterine scar.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and non-Hodgkin lymphoma
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split into two unrelated groups. One cluster captures epilepsy and focal/complex partial seizures, alongside Parkinsonian features such as tremor, abnormal involuntary movements, mild cognitive impairment, and REM sleep behavior disorder. The other cluster comprises non-Hodgkin B‑cell lymphomas (diffuse large B‑cell, follicular, marginal zone, nodal/extranodal).

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions are generic comorbidities and symptoms (hypertension, diabetes, GERD, obesity, pain) mixed with scattered unrelated diagnoses, including seizures and liver transplant complications. There is no cohesive clinical pattern to interpret.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine bipolar spectrum disorders with primary hyperparathyroidism and hypercalcemia. It also pulls in hidradenitis and pilonidal disease with keloid/hypertrophic scarring, plus cholelithiasis and gynecologic/breast diagnoses. These span unrelated organ systems without a single clinical pathway, so this grouping is not a usable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix substance‑related psychiatric diagnoses (psychosis, hallucinations, polysubstance use, suicidal/homicidal ideation) with scattered cardiopulmonary and pain entries (pneumothorax, pleural/ pericardial effusion, tachycardia, chest and abdominal pain), without a single clinical storyline. This pattern is diffuse and low‑signal, so it does not support a usable clinical phenotype for cohort definition.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathies and entrapments
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on peripheral nerve disorders: polyneuropathy (including idiopathic/toxic forms), mononeuritis and mononeuropathies, and entrapment syndromes such as carpal/tarsal tunnel, often coded with limb disorders and arthropathy. Cardiometabolic and gastrointestinal codes appear but the core signal is peripheral nervous system disease.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (e.g., hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, chest and abdominal pain, nausea) mixed with scattered unrelated rare items. There is no shared clinical theme, so this is not a usable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and common symptoms without a unifying clinical story. The rarer conditions are scattered across unrelated systems, so this does not form a usable clinical cluster.

phenotypes.label: Diabetic retinopathy and ocular comorbidities
phenotypes.description: Advanced diabetic eye disease, including macular edema, proliferative and nonproliferative retinopathy, retinal vascular occlusion, vitreous hemorrhage, cataract, and refractive or ocular surface disorders. Co-occurring erectile dysfunction with hypogonadism/hypopituitarism suggests systemic diabetic complications in a predominantly male subset.

phenotypes.label: Ocular diseases and pericardial disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between ophthalmic disease (retinal detachment, glaucoma, keratoconus, corneal ulcer, corneal transplant/pseudophakia) and pericardial disorders (acute pericarditis, pericardial effusion). These are unrelated organ systems without a single unifying clinical mechanism, indicating a merged theme rather than one phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder with recurrent UTI
phenotypes.description: Neurogenic bladder with recurrent urinary tract infection and catheter-related complications, often in the context of spinal cord injury or spina bifida. Associated findings include urinary retention, hydronephrosis, hematuria, pyelonephritis/urosepsis, bladder stones, and ureteral stricture, along with pressure ulcers and wheelchair dependence.

phenotypes.label: Gastric cancer with metastases
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on stomach malignancy with associated stomach disorders, highlighting advanced disease. Frequent findings include bone metastases, intra‑abdominal lymphadenopathy, pleural and pericardial effusions, pneumothorax, lung nodules, bowel obstruction, and multiple lesions—consistent with metastatic spread and thoracic involvement.

phenotypes.label: Mixed neuroendocrine tumors and dyslipidemia
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine gastrointestinal neuroendocrine/carcinoid tumors (including small‑intestine primaries and hepatic metastases) with a separate cluster of inherited lipid disorders and hereditary thrombophilias. Abdominal wall hernias and other disparate diagnoses are also present, with no single clinical pathway linking these themes.

phenotypes.label: Cardiometabolic heart disease baseline
phenotypes.description: A broad cluster of chronic cardiometabolic conditions—hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obesity, and sleep apnea—co-occurring with coronary atherosclerosis, atrial fibrillation, and heart failure. Diabetic complications including chronic kidney disease, neuropathy, and peripheral circulatory/dermopathy changes are common, reflecting prevalent cardiovascular disease in adults with metabolic syndrome. This represents common chronic comorbidity burden rather than a specific narrow phenotype.

phenotypes.label: End-stage liver disease and complications
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic liver disease with cirrhosis, often associated with hepatitis C, steatosis/NASH, or alcohol-related injury. Findings reflect portal hypertension and decompensation—ascites, varices, hepatic encephalopathy—along with cytopenias and splenomegaly, and complications such as portal vein thrombosis, spontaneous bacterial peritonitis, hepatocellular carcinoma, and prior liver transplant status.

phenotypes.label: Voice and laryngeal disorders with transplant
phenotypes.description: Two distinct patterns are present: a strong cluster of laryngeal/voice conditions (dysphonia, vocal fold paralysis, laryngeal edema and spasm, dysphagia) and a separate group of cardiac transplant–related conditions (transplant status, rejection, transplant vasculopathy) with hematologic disorders (acute myeloid leukemia, von Willebrand disease). These reflect unrelated organ systems and mechanisms, so this combines disparate themes rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis with VTE and osteoporosis
phenotypes.description: Seropositive and seronegative rheumatoid arthritis with polyarticular and hand involvement, synovitis, and erosive changes. Co-occurring deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism indicate venous thromboembolism risk, and osteoporosis with vertebral wedge fractures suggests inflammatory bone loss. Autoimmune-associated features such as dry eyes, oral ulcers, photosensitivity, and alopecia areata are also represented.

phenotypes.label: MS and neurodevelopmental neurologic disorders
phenotypes.description: Broad neurologic cluster combining multiple sclerosis and related demyelinating/neuro‑ophthalmic presentations (optic neuritis) with cranial nerve disorders (Bell’s palsy, hemifacial spasm, facial nerve disorders). It also captures neurodevelopmental diagnoses such as ADHD and autism, alongside anxiety and mood symptoms. This represents patients with central nervous system conditions spanning demyelinating disease and neurodevelopmental spectra.

phenotypes.label: Distal extremity pain and injuries
phenotypes.description: Distal extremity musculoskeletal conditions centered on foot and ankle pain, plantar fasciitis/fibromatosis, enthesopathy, tendinitis (tibialis, peroneal, Achilles), osteoarthritis, and deformities such as hallux valgus and flatfoot. Upper-limb counterparts including wrist and hand pain, tenosynovitis, carpal tunnel syndrome, trigger finger, and related fractures are also represented, reflecting overuse, degenerative change, and minor trauma affecting hands and feet.

phenotypes.label: HIV with herpesvirus dermatitis
phenotypes.description: Patients with HIV infection accompanied by prominent herpesvirus disease, especially herpes simplex and vesicular dermatitis. Associated findings point to opportunistic and dermatologic involvement with occasional ocular, salivary gland, genitourinary (hematuria), and hepatic manifestations (jaundice/bilirubin abnormalities).

phenotypes.label: Leukemia/lymphoma with HSCT complications
phenotypes.description: Hematologic malignancies with history of hematopoietic stem cell transplant and post‑transplant complications. Prominent features include graft‑versus‑host disease, chemotherapy‑related pancytopenia and thrombocytopenia with epistaxis, and opportunistic infections such as cytomegalovirus and gastrointestinal/viral enteritis. Central line infections and occasional ocular findings are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine common baseline comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD) with scattered unrelated problems (amputation, pancreatitis, DVT) and no shared mechanism. This does not form a coherent clinical cluster for phenotype selection.

phenotypes.label: Primary brain neoplasm and sequelae
phenotypes.description: Primary intracranial neoplasms, prominently frontal lobe tumors, with mass effect and neurologic sequelae. Findings include cerebral edema, obstructive hydrocephalus, papilledema, hemiplegia, headache, abnormal head imaging, postoperative state, and neutropenia; head and neck malignancies also co-occur.

phenotypes.label: Mixed CF/bronchiectasis and pituitary disorders
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated clusters are combined: a cystic fibrosis/bronchiectasis pattern with chronic pulmonary infection and exocrine pancreatic insufficiency, and a pituitary adenoma/hyperprolactinemia cluster. Additional neurologic pain diagnoses appear, so this does not form a single coherent clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all, GI skew
phenotypes.description: Common chronic conditions such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity/OSA, and mood disorders co-occur with prominent upper gastrointestinal and functional bowel diagnoses (GERD, gastritis, IBS, diverticular disease, hiatal hernia). This reflects a broad primary‑care baseline with dyspepsia and abdominal pain rather than a single focused disease entity.

phenotypes.label: COPD, HIV, and male GU disorders
phenotypes.description: This combines three unrelated themes: chronic obstructive lung disease, asymptomatic HIV/immunodeficiency, and a cluster of male genital disorders (hydrocele, varicocele, epididymitis, phimosis, scrotal pain). These conditions do not reflect a single clinical pathway and likely represent merged subgroups rather than one phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Recurrent major depression with anxiety
phenotypes.description: Patients with recurrent major depressive disorder across severities and remission states, often accompanied by generalized anxiety disorder. Includes depressive episodes and anxiety-spectrum diagnoses reflecting chronic mood–anxiety comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis and complications
phenotypes.description: Chronic ulcerative colitis across subtypes (pancolitis, proctitis, left-sided, rectosigmoiditis) with characteristic complications. Prominent features include lower GI bleeding (rectal hemorrhage, hematochezia), pseudopolyposis, diarrhea, anorectal disorders, and pouchitis.

phenotypes.label: Uterine and cervical malignancies
phenotypes.description: Gynecologic cancers centered on endometrial (uterine) and cervical neoplasms, alongside precursor lesions such as endometrial hyperplasia and endometrial intraepithelial neoplasia. Post-hysterectomy status and radiation-related complications (e.g., radiation proctitis/dermatitis) are common, with regional lymph node involvement noted.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis with internal derangement
phenotypes.description: Degenerative knee disease dominates: primary knee osteoarthritis with knee pain, effusion, and chondromalacia patella. Meniscal derangements and tears, cruciate ligament sprain, pes anserine bursitis, and loose bodies point to intra-articular mechanical pathology, with obesity, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia as common comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Inflammatory bowel disease with perianal complications
phenotypes.description: Inflammatory bowel disease centered on colonic involvement with prominent perianal disease is evident, including proctitis, perianal/rectal abscesses, and anal fistulas. Associated gastrointestinal features include malabsorption, anorectal disorders, skin tags, and postoperative syndromes.

phenotypes.label: Myasthenia gravis
phenotypes.description: Patients coded for myasthenia gravis, with prominent ocular and bulbar manifestations such as diplopia, dysarthria, dysphagia, and facial weakness. Neurological exam findings including muscle fasciculation and pronator drift co-occur, consistent with a neuromuscular junction disorder.

phenotypes.label: Actinic keratoses and keratinocyte carcinomas
phenotypes.description: Sun-exposed, photoaged patients with actinic keratoses, seborrheic keratoses, lentigines, melanocytic nevi, and skin lesions of uncertain behavior, with frequent basal and squamous cell skin cancers. Age-related ocular findings (presbyopia, astigmatism, cataract, vitreous degeneration) co-occur, reflecting cumulative UV exposure and aging.

phenotypes.label: Graves disease and thyrotoxicosis
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune hyperthyroidism consistent with Graves disease, with thyrotoxicosis, toxic diffuse goiter, and associated ophthalmopathy (exophthalmos). Prothrombotic conditions such as hypercoagulable states and antiphospholipid syndrome co-occur in this group, reflecting autoimmune and thrombotic risk.

phenotypes.label: Shoulder, Crohn's, and lung cancer
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split across unrelated areas: shoulder pain/rotator cuff and glenohumeral osteoarthritis; Crohn’s disease with complications such as fistula and obstruction; and lung malignancy with nodules, imaging findings, postoperative states, or lung absence. There is no shared clinical theme across these conditions.

phenotypes.label: Schizophrenia and schizoaffective spectrum
phenotypes.description: Psychotic disorders centered on schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder, including paranoid and chronic subtypes. Co‑occurring conditions include drug‑induced dyskinesia and blepharospasm, visual symptoms and glaucoma suspect, and metabolic comorbidity such as impaired glucose tolerance/diabetes, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia.

phenotypes.label: Sarcoidosis with ocular involvement
phenotypes.description: Patients with sarcoidosis (including pulmonary/cutaneous) accompanied by clustered ophthalmic findings—retinal vascular/retinal disorders, vitreous opacities/degeneration, optic nerve changes, glaucoma suspect, cataract, and meibomian gland dysfunction—consistent with ocular sarcoidosis. Sexual activity and anal dysplasia codes appear in the records, but the dominant pattern is sarcoid‑associated eye disease.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span unrelated systems without a single clinical thread. Benign and neoplastic breast disease (fibrocystic change, DCIS) and renal/ovarian cysts appear alongside neurologic entities such as acoustic neuroma, viral meningitis, and acute infective polyneuritis (Guillain–Barré syndrome). Additional acute problems like gastritis, appendicitis, and patellar fracture reinforce that this is a merged set rather than one phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Dementia with comorbid bipolar disorder
phenotypes.description: Neurocognitive disorders dominate, including Alzheimer’s disease, vascular dementia, and other dementias with behavioral disturbance, accompanied by impaired cognition, visual hallucinations, and mobility limitations. Bipolar I disorder with depressive episodes is frequently co-reported, and some patients have embolic stroke, consistent with a dementia spectrum with mood comorbidity and vascular contributions.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathy with foot drop
phenotypes.description: Peripheral nerve and neuromuscular disorders with prominent inflammatory/toxic neuropathy, common peroneal nerve lesion, and distal lower‑limb involvement causing foot‑drop and numbness. Frequent pharyngeal dysfunction and sleep‑related hypoventilation indicate bulbar and respiratory muscle weakness in some patients, with co‑occurring sleep apnea and organic insomnia. Hematologic malignancy appears in a subset, consistent with neuropathy occurring with systemic disease.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer with thyroidectomy sequelae
phenotypes.description: Represents thyroid carcinoma and nodular thyroid disease. Many patients have undergone thyroidectomy or ablation with postoperative hypothyroidism, with complications such as hypoparathyroidism and hypocalcemia, and recurrent laryngeal nerve injury presenting as vocal cord paralysis or dysphonia.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions reflect generic primary‑care complaints and common comorbidities (anxiety, depression, abdominal pain, headache, upper respiratory infection) without a single unifying disease process. Scattered gynecologic findings appear only sparsely and do not form a coherent clinical cluster.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span unrelated clinical areas. They include ear/vestibular disorders (sensorineural hearing loss, Ménière’s disease, tinnitus, vertigo), adrenal gland disorders and benign neoplasms with hypercortisolism, menopausal symptoms/postmenopausal bleeding and dyspareunia, hematologic diseases (polycythemia vera, thalassemia), and colon polyp and salivary gland disorders. These represent separate organ systems and mechanisms, so this pattern is not a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psychiatric, dermatologic, and vestibular mix
phenotypes.description: The top conditions blend mood/anxiety and obsessive–compulsive spectrum disorders with inflammatory skin diseases such as acne and hidradenitis, plus self-excoriation. It also pulls in ear/vestibular problems (sudden hearing loss, vertigo) and spasmodic torticollis, which are not part of the same pathway. Overall this is a mixture of unrelated themes rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Allergic rhinitis–sinusitis with asthma
phenotypes.description: Patients show atopic upper and lower airway disease: allergic rhinitis (including pollen and animal dander triggers) with chronic rhinosinusitis, nasal polyps, turbinate hypertrophy, and structural nasal variants, alongside asthma including eosinophilic/IgE‑mediated forms. Symptoms such as nasal congestion and cough reflect a united airway inflammatory phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Pelvic organ prolapse and incontinence
phenotypes.description: Female pelvic floor disorders characterized by pelvic organ prolapse (uterovaginal prolapse, cystocele, rectocele) with stress, urge, and mixed urinary incontinence and overactive bladder symptoms (urgency, frequency, nocturia). Atrophic vaginitis and pelvic floor dysfunction are frequent comorbidities, with occasional fecal incontinence and pelvic pain.

phenotypes.label: End-stage renal disease and transplant
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic kidney disease progressing to end-stage renal disease with hypertensive kidney disease, proteinuria, and stages 3–5, accompanied by anemia of CKD, hyperparathyroidism/renal osteodystrophy, and hyperkalemia. Many patients have renal transplant status and graft complications (disorder, rejection, failure), with immunodeficiency and postoperative state consistent with post‑transplant immunosuppression; arteriovenous fistula and glomerular disease further anchor this renal phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Sleep apnea, hypersomnia, and RLS
phenotypes.description: Clinical cluster of sleep disorders including obstructive sleep apnea, hypersomnia, and narcolepsy, often accompanied by restless legs and periodic limb movements. Cognitive and attentional complaints and headache syndromes (migraine and idiopathic intracranial hypertension) co-occur, with some cases linked to head injury or CSF leak and associated smell/taste disturbances.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic cardiometabolic comorbidities (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, hyperlipidemia, obesity) with assorted symptoms and complications. There is no single, distinct clinical pattern tying these together into a focused phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix essential tremor and other movement symptoms with unrelated colorectal polyps/hemorrhoidal tags, limb melanomas, fractures, and hematologic disorders. This blend lacks a single clinical mechanism and does not represent one coherent condition.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between type 1 diabetes with its complications (hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia, DKA, neuropathy, retinopathy) and malignant neoplasms (prostate cancer with metastatic spread, bladder and pancreatic cancers, cancer‑related pain/fatigue). These are unrelated clinical pathways without a single unifying mechanism, so this cluster does not represent one coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This set combines venous thromboembolism and thrombophilia (deep venous thrombosis, lupus anticoagulant, prothrombin mutation, splenic infarction) with unrelated facial trauma/soft‑tissue conditions and eye injuries. It also includes anogenital HPV and cervical dysplasia and vaginitis. These represent distinct, unrelated clinical themes rather than one condition or pathway.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis with psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: Psoriatic disease with cutaneous psoriasis and joint involvement (psoriatic arthropathy/psoriatic arthritis). Nail involvement (onycholysis) and dactylitis are present, with additional axial and peripheral arthritis codes reflecting the spondyloarthritis spectrum.

phenotypes.label: Sexual health, mood, and skin mix
phenotypes.description: The conditions combine sexual health documentation (contraception use, sexually active with men, trichomonas), mood and depressive disorders with screening results, and dermatologic/foot problems (nail dystrophy, dermatophytosis, ingrown nail, pruritic rash) with gait difficulty. These represent distinct care domains without a single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Uncomplicated herpes zoster
phenotypes.description: Patients coded for herpes zoster without complications, consistent with shingles episodes. Secondary codes are sparse and heterogeneous (e.g., dysphagia or pharyngeal disorders), but the pattern is driven by uncomplicated zoster.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading items are broad, nonspecific complaints (pain, nausea, chest pain, shortness of breath, vomiting, diarrhea) mixed with scattered unrelated findings. There is no unifying diagnosis or coherent clinical pattern here, so it is not clinically actionable.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine insomnia and sleep initiation/maintenance problems with otologic disorders such as sensorineural hearing loss, tympanic membrane perforation, dizziness, and asymmetric hearing loss. Additional disparate features, including dystonia, jaw region inflammation, syncope, and transient visual loss, indicate there is no single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: SLE/Sjögren’s with interstitial lung disease
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune connective tissue disease cluster centered on systemic lupus, with frequent Sjögren’s features (keratoconjunctivitis sicca), Raynaud’s, inflammatory polyarthropathy, and myositis-spectrum diagnoses. Many records show interstitial lung disease and pulmonary fibrosis, along with lupus nephritis, indicating multisystem involvement.

phenotypes.label: Hepatitis B and spondyloarthritis
phenotypes.description: This combines two unrelated patterns. One half centers on chronic hepatitis B with hepatobiliary findings such as steatosis and gallbladder cholesterolosis/polyps. The other half reflects inflammatory rheumatologic disease (ankylosing spondylitis, arteritis, panniculitis), so there is no single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Neurofibromatosis and psychiatric/pulmonary mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine neurofibromatosis (including NF1 and autonomic nerve tumors) and a site-specific breast cancer cluster with ocular findings, alongside unrelated schizoaffective disorder and tobacco-related chronic bronchitis. This spans distinct body systems without a unifying clinical pathway, so it reflects mixed themes rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed CNS–endocrine and viral exanthems
phenotypes.description: This blends central nervous system neoplasms and pituitary disorders (meningioma/benign brain tumor, hypopituitarism, diabetes insipidus) alongside narcolepsy. It also pulls in unrelated atopic and respiratory diagnoses and childhood viral exanthems (varicella, measles, mumps, rubella). These represent unrelated mechanisms, indicating merged themes rather than a single clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Long COVID and personality disorders
phenotypes.description: Combines post-acute COVID-19 with dissociative identity and borderline personality disorders. These represent unrelated clinical themes without a unifying condition, with additional scattered musculoskeletal, neurologic, and obstetric diagnoses, so this is not a single coherent phenotype.
